data.title: Unstructured awarded $2M by AFWERX to enhance AI data pipelines and testing frameworks for US Air Force.
data.description: Unstructured, based in San Francisco, has recently been granted a $2 million Tactical Funding Increase (TACFI) contract by AFWERX alongside the U.S. Air Force Test Center’s (AFTC) 96th Test Wing. This collaboration builds upon previous endeavors in the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Phase II program. The awarded funding will be utilized to develop sophisticated multimodal data pipelines essential for driving generative AI-enabled testing tools. Moreover, the project aims to create robust test and evaluation frameworks that enhance confidence in AI applications within the organization.

data.title: Cann's social tonics now available at Spec's Wine, Spirits & Finer Foods in Texas. Includes 2mg, 5mg, and 10mg THC products.
data.description: In Dallas, Cann, a leading brand known for its popular THC-infused social tonics, has revealed a significant retail collaboration with Spec's Wine, Spirits & Finer Foods, Texas' biggest beverage seller. As of this month, Spec's will stock Cann's acclaimed range of low-dose drinks statewide. Customers can expect to see Cann's well-known 2mg, 5mg, and 10mg THC social tonics, convenient liquid packets, Cann Spritz, and assorted packs in 2mg and 10mg versions on shelves.

data.title: Airspan Networks launches its MobileAccess Digital DAS platform across Europe and the UK, expanding international growth strategy.
data.description: Airspan Networks Holdings LLC, a prominent wireless network solutions provider, has introduced its MobileAccess Digital DAS platform in Europe and the UK to enhance its global expansion efforts. The MobileAccess portfolio, already established in the US, has gained approval from major mobile network operators and is utilized by top third-party operators and Fortune 1000 enterprises in sectors like healthcare and transportation. This move signifies Airspan's commitment to leveraging its successful platform on an international scale.

data.title: SpendHound recognized on G2's 2026 Best Software Awards for Best IT Management Software Products, reaching over 100 million buyers.
data.description: SpendHound, a subsidiary of YipitData which offers transparency into software spending for finance teams, has been recognized on G2's 2026 Best Software Awards. This places SpendHound on the list of Best IT Management Software Products. G2, the leading software marketplace globally, connects with over 100 million buyers each year. The Best Software Awards, conducted annually by G2, evaluate the top software companies and products worldwide using genuine, current feedback from actual users.

data.title: Peter Thiel and Founders Fund sell all ETHZilla shares, holding 0.0% as of Dec. 31, according to SEC filing.
data.description: Peter Thiel and his Founders Fund have sold off their entire stake in ETHZilla Corporation (NASDAQ: ETHZ), as reported in an amended Schedule 13G filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Tuesday. The filing, signed by Thiel on the same day, indicates that all entities associated with the Founders Fund now possess no shares of ETHZ common stock as of December 31. The SEC document identifies six reporting individuals connected to this divestment: FF Consumer Growth II, LP; The Founders Fund Growth II Management, LP; FF Upper Tier GP, LLC; FF Consumer Growth, LLC; The Founders Fund Growth Management, LLC; and Peter Thiel himself. For more details, the full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
China Coast Guard (CCG) spotted a Coast Guard vessel discarding unidentified objects into the South China Sea on Monday, according to newly released footage.

data.title: Global Payments reported Q4 2025 results, with adjusted earnings per share at $3.18 beating estimates. Revenue was $2.32 billion, slightly below estimates. Adjusted net revenues increased by 1%, and the company unveiled a $2.5 billion stock buyback.
data.description: Global Payments Inc. (NYSE: GPN) saw an increase in its stock on Wednesday following the release of its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results. Adjusted earnings per share rose by 12% year over year to $3.18, exceeding the analyst consensus of $3.16. The company reported revenue of $2.320 billion, slightly below the analyst estimate of $2.325 billion. Adjusted net revenues increased by 1% year over year (6% on a constant-currency basis excluding dispositions). The adjusted operating margin also grew by 80 basis points to 44.7% in the quarter. Global Payments Inc. finalized the acquisition of Worldpay and the divestiture of Issuer Solutions, repositioning itself as a premier pure-play merchant solutions provider. Following its divestiture announcement, Issuer Solutions was classified as discontinued operations effective in the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: Logile launches "Storefront Science" podcast in Dallas, featuring industry experts to decode retail operations with AI.
data.description: Logile, Inc., a prominent player in AI-driven Connected Workforce solutions, launched its podcast Storefront Science in Dallas. The podcast features in-depth discussions with industry experts, shedding light on the intricate retail operation economics. It explores how AI, unified workforce tools, and operational excellence impact tangible business outcomes. Hosted by Logile's Ryan Whitfield and Ben Hinshaw, Storefront Science addresses key challenges in the retail sector, aiming to educate listeners on impactful strategies and technologies.

data.title: LandGate introduces Battery Storage Analysis tool; automates engineering-grade Due Diligence for real estate & energy.
data.description: In Denver on February 18, 2026, LandGate, a prominent data intelligence company in the energy and real estate sectors, unveiled its latest innovation - the Battery Storage Analysis tool. This cutting-edge technology is the first of its kind and offers automated, engineering-grade Battery Storage Due Diligence.

data.title: Investors advised of April 6, 2026 deadline by Berger Montague for class action lawsuit against Mereo BioPharma Group (NASDAQ: MREO).
data.description: A class action lawsuit has been initiated by national plaintiffs' law firm Berger Montague PC against Mereo BioPharma Group plc (NASDAQ: MREO) on behalf of investors who bought American Depositary Shares. The lawsuit, filed in Philadelphia on February 18, 2026, targets Mereo for alleged wrongdoing related to its securities. The precise number of affected investors and the total value involved have not been specified, but the case focuses on those who purchased Mereo's shares during a certain period. The legal action underscores the ongoing scrutiny and potential financial implications facing the Company.

data.title: Dow gains 100 points to 49,637.82. Analog Devices beats Q1 earnings, $2.46 EPS and $3.16B revenue. Energy ↑ 1.3%.
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with gains as the Dow Jones index rose 0.21% to 49,637.82 points, with the NASDAQ up 0.17% at 22,616.77 points and the S&P 500 increasing by 0.19% to 6,855.97 points on Wednesday. Energy shares surged by 1.3%, while real estate stocks dropped by 0.8%. Analog Devices Inc (NASDAQ: ADI) reported Q1 earnings of $2.46 per share, beating the estimate of $2.31 per share, with quarterly sales at $3.160 billion, surpassing the estimate of $3.117 billion. Sensei Biotherapeutics Inc (NASDAQ: SNSE) saw its shares spike 230% to $30.12. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Tanium appoints Adam Ostopowich as Canada country manager to lead unified national sales structure for greater customer support.
data.description: In Emeryville, California, Tanium, a prominent figure in Autonomous IT, revealed plans to streamline its Canadian sales operations by instituting a unified, nationally overseen structure. Adam Ostopowich has been appointed as the country manager for Canada to spearhead this effort. This strategic move exemplifies Tanium’s enduring commitment to the Canadian market and its dedication to providing enhanced support to customers and partners through teams equipped with extensive knowledge of the Canadian landscape. This revamped approach aims to foster better cohesion within Tanium’s Canadian business, catering to mid-market needs while demonstrating a focus on unified operations.
